title_en: Using Wafayat al-A'yan Today: Editions, Translations, and Research Value
content_en:
Wafayat al-A'yan remains an active research tool for scholars of Islamic intellectual history, Arabic literature, and the medieval Islamic world. Its combination of reliable dates, literary quality, and breadth of coverage makes it indispensable for researchers working on any figure who lived between the second and seventh Islamic centuries.

The critical edition by Ihsan Abbas, published by Dar Sadir in Beirut in eight volumes (1968–1972), is the standard reference for scholarly citations. This edition includes a thorough index and is well produced, making it relatively easy to navigate despite its size. Various reprints of this edition are commercially available from publishers in Lebanon, Egypt, and elsewhere.

For researchers without Arabic, de Slane's French translation remains the primary resource for the complete text, though it is dated and some of its transliterations and identifications need updating with modern scholarship. Partial English translations and translations of specific entries exist in academic articles and dissertations but no complete English translation has been produced.

For students of Islamic intellectual history, a productive approach involves using the Wafayat as a secondary biographical resource alongside the primary sources of the figures being studied. When researching a jurist, poet, or historian, checking the Wafayat entry provides a medieval scholarly assessment and often preserves details and anecdotes not found elsewhere. Cross-referencing with adh-Dhahabi's Siyar Alam an-Nubala and as-Safadi's Al-Wafi bil-Wafayat typically rounds out the biographical picture.

For students of Arabic literature, the Wafayat's extensive poetry quotations make it a valuable anthology of classical Arabic verse. Ibn Khallikan's literary judgments, while those of a medieval Shafi'i scholar rather than a modern literary critic, are informed and worth engaging with seriously.

The Wafayat al-A'yan models an approach to scholarly biography that remains relevant: taking intellectual achievement seriously as historical evidence, contextualizing individuals within their scholarly networks, and maintaining honesty about the limits of available information. These habits, cultivated by Ibn Khallikan eight centuries ago, continue to define good practice in Islamic biographical scholarship.
title_id: Menggunakan Wafayat al-A'yan Hari Ini: Edisi, Terjemahan, dan Nilai Penelitian
content_id:
Wafayat al-A'yan tetap menjadi alat penelitian aktif bagi para ulama sejarah intelektual Islam, sastra Arab, dan dunia Islam abad pertengahan. Perpaduan antara tanggal-tanggal yang andal, kualitas sastra, dan keluasan cakupannya menjadikannya tidak tergantikan bagi para peneliti yang mengkaji tokoh-tokoh yang hidup antara abad kedua dan ketujuh Hijriah.

Edisi kritis oleh Ihsan Abbas, diterbitkan oleh Dar Sadir di Beirut dalam delapan jilid (1968–1972), adalah referensi standar untuk kutipan keilmuan. Edisi ini mencakup indeks yang lengkap dan diproduksi dengan baik, sehingga relatif mudah dinavigasi meskipun ukurannya besar. Berbagai cetakan ulang dari edisi ini tersedia secara komersial dari penerbit di Lebanon, Mesir, dan tempat-tempat lainnya.

Bagi para peneliti yang tidak menguasai bahasa Arab, terjemahan Prancis de Slane tetap menjadi sumber daya utama untuk teks lengkap, meskipun sudah kuno dan beberapa transliterasi serta identifikasinya perlu diperbarui dengan keilmuan modern. Terjemahan Inggris parsial dan terjemahan entri-entri tertentu ada dalam artikel akademis dan disertasi, namun tidak ada terjemahan Inggris lengkap yang pernah diproduksi.

Bagi para pelajar sejarah intelektual Islam, pendekatan yang produktif melibatkan penggunaan Wafayat sebagai sumber daya biografi sekunder bersama sumber-sumber primer tokoh-tokoh yang sedang dikaji. Ketika meneliti seorang ahli hukum, penyair, atau sejarawan, memeriksa entri Wafayat memberikan penilaian keilmuan abad pertengahan dan sering kali melestarikan detail dan anekdot yang tidak ditemukan di tempat lain. Referensi silang dengan Siyar A'lam an-Nubala karya adz-Dzahabi dan Al-Wafi bil-Wafayat karya as-Safadi biasanya melengkapi gambaran biografi.

Bagi para pelajar sastra Arab, kutipan-kutipan puisi yang ekstensif di seluruh karya menjadikannya sebuah antologi berharga dari syair Arab klasik dan panduan menuju kehidupan para penyair utama.

Its combination of reliable dates, literary quality, and breadth of coverage makes it indispensable for researchers working on any figure who lived between the ثانياً and سابعاً Islamic centuries. The البالغ الأهمية edition by Ihsan Abbas, published by Dar Sadir in Beirut in eight volumes (1968–1972), is the standard reference for العلمي citations. This edition includes a thorough index and is well produced, making it relatively easy to navigate رغم its size. Various reprints of this edition are commercially available from publishers in Lebanon, Egypt, and elsewhere. For researchers without Arabic, de Slane's French translation remains the الأساسي resource for the complete text, though it is dated and some of its transliterations and identifications need updating with modern scholarship. Partial English translations and translations of specific entries exist in الأكاديمي articles and dissertations but no complete English translation has been produced. For students of Islamic intellectual history, a productive approach involves using the Wafayat as a secondary biographical resource alongside the الأساسي sources of the figures being studied. عندما researching a jurist, poet, or historian, checking the Wafayat entry provides a medieval العلمي assessment and often preserves details and anecdotes not found elsewhere. Cross-referencing with adh-Dhahabi's Siyar Alam an-Nubala and as-Safadi's Al-Wafi bil-Wafayat typically rounds out the biographical picture. For students of Arabic literature, the Wafayat's extensive poetry quotations make it a valuable anthology of الكلاسيكي Arabic الآية. Ibn Khallikan's literary judgments, بينما those of a medieval الشافعي scholar rather than a modern literary critic, are informed and worth engaging with seriously. The Wafayat al-A'yan models an approach to العلمي biography that remains relevant: taking intellectual achievement seriously as historical evidence, contextualizing individuals within their العلمي networks, and maintaining honesty about the limits of available information. These habits, cultivated by Ibn Khallikan eight centuries ago, continue to define الخير practice in Islamic biographical scholarship.
